title: antiaris toxicaria subsp. humbertii
intro: Antiaris toxicaria subsp. humbertii, also known as the Madagascar Tox Tree, is a subspecies of the Antiaris toxicaria plant, a species of flowering plant in the family Moraceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with the plant include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a natural remedy for wounds and insect bites. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, which are applied topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
